goal: Land the test-runner-mcp (G8 Testing+CI, P0 priority) external MCP plugin manifest using the DEC-0025-corrected upstream path: `npx -y @iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner@0.2.1` (the third-party scoped mirror; SHA `d6ccbd99f3c9b599216e3d9f655b6cb22e33867f` for defensive pinning), with a real-subprocess `tools/list` health-check integration test asserting (a) the canonical single-tool dispatcher `run_tests` is advertised AND (b) the tool's `framework` enum exposes ≥6 framework values from the canonical set {bats, pytest, flutter, jest, go, rust, cargo, generic, vitest}. Re-emits the archived WO-0081 with all DEC-0024 source-data errors corrected per DEC-0025 (bare `test-runner-mcp` is NOT on npm — 404; upstream advertises ONE `run_tests` tool with framework enum, NOT 6 separate tools). Tier-2 vendored TS wrapper is OUT OF SCOPE per DEC-0025 §Decision rejection of Option B; revisit only via fresh ADR if `@iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner` rots.
plan_summary: Solo-feature G8 plugin-manifest landing WO mirroring the WO-0080 ruff revival shape, retargeted from G7-quality+PyPI/uvx to G8-testing+npm/npx with the single-tool-dispatcher pattern (NOT the 6-tool surface DEC-0024 incorrectly prescribed). Add plugins/testing/test-runner/plugin.toml + scripts/devtools/install-test-runner-mcp.sh + replacement scripts/verify/P3-W11-F07.sh + ONE new test `test_runner_manifest_health_check` inserted into the existing `mod g8_plugin_manifests { ... }` block in crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs (additive only — DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out for the small ~80-LOC commit). Install path: `npx -y @iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner@0.2.1` (SHA `d6ccbd99f3c9b599216e3d9f655b6cb22e33867f`) per DEC-0025 §Decision. The cargo-test load-bearing assertion is `health.tools` contains exactly ONE entry named `run_tests`; the verify script's tool-level smoke (independent JSON-RPC tools/list capture) inspects the inputSchema's `framework` enum and asserts ≥6 framework values are present. Pre-baked M1/M2 mutation contract per the 9-WO mature pattern (WO-0066/0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0077/0079/0080). F07 attempts=0 — first executor cycle (the WO-0081 executor halted with empirical-validation escalation, not a verifier rejection; per DEC-0025 §Consequences attempts counter remains 0).
scope_in: 1. PRIMARY install path per DEC-0025 §Decision point 1: `npx -y @iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner@0.2.1` (the only published surface; SHA-pinned to `d6ccbd99f3c9b599216e3d9f655b6cb22e33867f` per master-plan §13 + DEC-0025 §Rationale). The `<PINNED-REF>` is FROZEN at `0.2.1` for this WO (DEC-0025 §Decision selected Option A with explicit version pin). Floating refs (`@latest`, `@main`, no version) are FORBIDDEN per AC15 + master-plan §13. The bare `test-runner-mcp` name returns 404 from npm per DEC-0025 §Context Error 1 — DO NOT attempt to use it.

2. Tier-2 vendored TS wrapper is OUT OF SCOPE per DEC-0025 §Decision point 4: explicitly REJECTING Option B (200+ LOC net-new TS infrastructure that no other UCIL plugin manifest carries). Master-plan §4.8 line 405 'unified test execution covers Rust, Python, TypeScript via MCP' is functional NOT structural and is satisfied by the iflow-mcp `run_tests` dispatcher with `framework={rust,pytest,jest}`. If the Tier-1 install fails at executor time, escalate (do NOT silently pivot to Tier-2 — that requires a fresh ADR superseding DEC-0025).

3. Create plugins/testing/test-runner/plugin.toml mirroring the structure of plugins/testing/mcp-pytest-runner/plugin.toml (P3-W11-F08 / WO-0077): leading multi-line comment with `pinned 2026-05-08:` block citing DEC-0025 (source URL = https://github.com/privsim/mcp-test-runner upstream of record; npm URL = https://www.npmjs.com/package/@iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner; pinned semver = `0.2.1`; pinned SHA = `d6ccbd99f3c9b599216e3d9f655b6cb22e33867f`; install command = `npx -y @iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner@0.2.1`; advertised tools list = `["run_tests"]` with count=1; framework enum = `["bats", "pytest", "flutter", "jest", "go", "rust", "generic"]` with count=7; transport=stdio; operator-state requirements section = NONE for the tools/list handshake — the SECONDARY operator-state CLIs `cargo`/`pytest`/`vitest`/`go`/`bats`/`flutter`/`jest` are tool-call-time NOT initialize-time deps; no parent-process env-var mutation needed); `[plugin]` section (name='test-runner' to match `serverInfo.name="test-runner"` per the live capture in DEC-0025 §Context, version='0.2.1', description citing master-plan §4.8 line 405 + §5.3 G8 source designation + 'multi-language unified test execution via MCP — single-tool dispatcher with framework enum per DEC-0025'); `[capabilities]` section with provides=['testing.run'] (single-dispatcher pattern; framework selection happens via the `framework` input-arg at tool-call time — capabilities namespace is established as one entry); languages=['rust', 'python', 'typescript', 'javascript', 'go', 'shell', 'dart'] (covers the 7-value framework enum: bats→shell, pytest→python, flutter→dart, jest→javascript+typescript, go→go, rust→rust, generic→[any]); `[transport]` section with type='stdio', command='npx', args=['-y', '@iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner@0.2.1']; `[lifecycle]` section mirroring F08 exactly (hot_cold=true, idle_timeout_minutes=10).

4. The `transport.command` field MUST be the bare binary name `"npx"` — NOT an absolute path — per WO-0069 lessons §executor: the M1 transport.command-poison mutation pattern relies on swapping to `/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__`, AND absolute paths break across CI/dev environments.

5. Live capture verification BEFORE sealing the manifest: drive the MCP `initialize` → `notifications/initialized` → `tools/list` handshake via a one-shot Python driver script (mirror the WO-0067/0069/0075/0076/0077/0079/0080/0081 `/tmp/wo-NNNN-capture.py` shape), against `npx -y @iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner@0.2.1`. Confirm the live capture matches DEC-0025 §Context expectations: (a) `tools[]` contains exactly 1 entry, (b) `tools[0].name == "run_tests"`, (c) `tools[0].inputSchema.properties.framework.enum` is `["bats", "pytest", "flutter", "jest", "go", "rust", "generic"]`, (d) `serverInfo.name == "test-runner"`, (e) `serverInfo.version == "0.1.0"`. If the live capture diverges from DEC-0025 §Context (e.g. the iflow-mcp mirror has been republished with a different surface), HALT and escalate — do NOT silently update the WO's expected literals (a divergence indicates the upstream rotted and a fresh ADR is needed). Capture transcript saved to `/tmp/wo-0082-capture.out` for the RFR.

6. Operator-state classification per WO-0076/0077 §planner lesson `distinguish operator-state-CLI-dependent vs self-resolving MCP servers`: @iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner is **SELF-RESOLVING at the MCP-server level** (npx-managed package handles Node + @modelcontextprotocol/sdk + the wrapper's own deps). The wrapper INTERNALLY shells out to `cargo`/`pytest`/`vitest`/`go`/`bats`/`flutter`/`jest` at tool-call time only — those constitute SECONDARY operator-state CLI dependencies that are NOT initialize-time requirements. The cargo-test (initialize-time `tools/list` handshake) requires NONE of them. The verify script's tool-level smoke (per scope_in #11) is OPTIONAL and gated by `cargo --version` short-circuit (operator-state pattern from WO-0076 Semgrep). Single env-gate: `if [[ "${UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E:-0}" != "0" ]]; then echo '[SKIP] P3-W11-F07: tool-level smoke skipped via UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E'; exit 0; fi` — extends the existing G8 env-var from WO-0077 verbatim. NB: WO-0076 §executor lesson `unsafe std::env::set_var('SEMGREP_PATH', ...)` does NOT apply here — test-runner-mcp needs no parent-process env-var mutation.

7. Extend crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs by ADDING a second test `test_runner_manifest_health_check` INSIDE the existing `mod g8_plugin_manifests { ... }` block (between the closing brace of the existing `mcp_pytest_runner_manifest_health_check` and the module's closing `}`). Mirror the existing test's shape exactly: `#[tokio::test] async fn`, `if skip_via_env() { return; }`, load manifest via `PluginManifest::from_path`, manifest sanity asserts (name='test-runner', transport.kind='stdio', !provides.is_empty(), provides.iter().all(|c| c.starts_with("testing."))), `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout(&manifest, FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS)`, assert health.name='test-runner' (matches `serverInfo.name` per DEC-0025 §Context), health.status=Ok, health.tools.len() == 1 (single-tool dispatcher per DEC-0025 §Decision point 3), AND a load-bearing assertion that `health.tools[0].name == "run_tests"` (the canonical single tool from the live capture). The framework-enum-coverage assertion (≥6 values from {bats, pytest, flutter, jest, go, rust, cargo, generic, vitest}) lives in the verify script (independent JSON-RPC capture inspecting inputSchema), NOT in the cargo test — `health.tools` from `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout` exposes the tool name list but not necessarily the full inputSchema (mirror WO-0077 mcp-pytest-runner test which asserts on tool name only).

8. ADDITIVELY only — do NOT modify the existing `mcp_pytest_runner_manifest_health_check` test inside `mod g8_plugin_manifests { ... }`. Confirm via `git diff main -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs | grep -E '^-' | grep -vE '^---' | wc -l` returning 0 (zero deletions; only added lines). The leading file-level rustdoc comment block may be amended ONLY to update the file-level overview: (a) change `mcp-pytest-runner G8` → `mcp-pytest-runner + test-runner G8` in the §Tests-wrapped paragraph, (b) replace the historical 'test-runner-mcp (P3-W11-F07) deferred per DEC-0021' note with 'test-runner-mcp (P3-W11-F07) revived per DEC-0024+DEC-0025 — single-tool dispatcher, npx -y @iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner@0.2.1', (c) preserve all WO-0076/0075/0077 carry-forward citations verbatim.

9. The new test lives INSIDE the existing `mod g8_plugin_manifests { ... }` block at module root (DEC-0007 frozen-selector module-root placement, carried per WO-0068 lessons §planner 'frozen-test selector substring-match REQUIRES module-root placement'). Confirm `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g8_plugin_manifests g8_plugin_manifests::test_runner_manifest_health_check` resolves uniquely (substring-matches without requiring `--exact`).

Create scripts/devtools/install-test-runner-mcp.sh mirroring scripts/devtools/install-mcp-pytest-runner.sh + scripts/devtools/install-eslint-mcp.sh verbatim in shape: `set -euo pipefail`, `PINNED_TEST_RUNNER_MCP_VERSION="0.2.1"` constant, `PINNED_TEST_RUNNER_MCP_PACKAGE="@iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner"` constant, OK-when-npx-on-PATH branch, MISSING branch with the exact pinned install command (`npx -y @iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner@0.2.1 --help` for the warm-up; `--help` may not be honored by the MCP server — that's fine, the npx fetch primes the cache as a side-effect anyway). Always `exit 0` (informational scaffolding — same contract as WO-0069/0072/0075/0076/0077/0079/0080).

11. Replace scripts/verify/P3-W11-F07.sh (currently TODO-stub returning exit 1) by copying scripts/verify/P3-W11-F08.sh OR scripts/verify/P3-W11-F03.sh verbatim and adapting: change pinned-ref constant to `@iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner@0.2.1`, change the cargo-test selector to `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g8_plugin_manifests g8_plugin_manifests::test_runner_manifest_health_check`, and add a NEW load-bearing tool-level smoke section (per DEC-0025 §Decision point 3 acceptance criterion #4) that performs an INDEPENDENT JSON-RPC `tools/list` capture (Python driver script in `mktemp -d` — same shape as `/tmp/wo-0082-capture.py` from scope_in #5) and asserts: (a) `tools[]` length == 1, (b) `tools[0].name == "run_tests"`, (c) `tools[0].inputSchema.properties.framework.enum` is a list with len ≥6, (d) the enum is a subset of the canonical set `{bats, pytest, flutter, jest, go, rust, cargo, generic, vitest}` (allows future upstream additions, asserts no foreign values). Single-gate `UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E` short-circuit per scope_in #6 (extends WO-0077 verbatim). OPTIONAL secondary tool-call-time smoke `tools/call run_tests {framework: "rust", ...}` against a workspace crate — gated by `cargo --version` short-circuit (skip on missing cargo); informational-only on green/skip. Print `[OK] P3-W11-F07` on green; `[FAIL] P3-W11-F07: <reason>` on red. Always `exit 0` on green / `exit 1` on red. The script remains read-only against tests/fixtures/** (forbidden_paths invariant).

12. Pre-baked mutation contract M1 (test-runner transport.command poison): swap `command = "npx"` → `command = "/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__"` in plugins/testing/test-runner/plugin.toml; cargo test selector `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g8_plugin_manifests g8_plugin_manifests::test_runner_manifest_health_check` must FAIL with `PluginError::Spawn { source: Os { code: 2, kind: NotFound } }` (or the analogous spawn-error chain on the platform); restore via `git checkout -- plugins/testing/test-runner/plugin.toml`; confirm md5sum matches the pre-mutation snapshot.

13. Pre-baked mutation contract M2 (expected-tool-name regression): in tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs's new `test_runner_manifest_health_check` test, change the `t == "run_tests"` assertion to a non-existent tool name (e.g., `t == "NON_EXISTENT_TOOL_M2"`); the test MUST FAIL with the structured panic carrying the live `health.tools` list (the `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...` panic body itself doubles as a diagnostic dump of the live tool surface at the pinned ref). Restore via `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs`; confirm md5sum match.

14. Pre-flight word-ban grep MUST cover ONLY production-side files (manifest, install script, verify script) and EXEMPT integration-test files under `#[cfg(test)]`/`tests/`. The grep `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' plugins/testing/test-runner/plugin.toml scripts/devtools/install-test-runner-mcp.sh scripts/verify/P3-W11-F07.sh` must return empty before first push. The integration-test file's rustdoc may mention 'Mocking ... is forbidden' inside a forbidden-pattern declaration (per WO-0048 line 363 + WO-0069 carve-out).

15. Pinned-immutable-ref policy: confirm `! grep -qE '"(main|latest|master|head|develop|dev|nightly)"' plugins/testing/test-runner/plugin.toml` returns empty (the floating-ref class). Frozen semver release tag `@0.2.1` is acceptable per DEC-0025 §Decision point 1 + master-plan §13. Bare branch refs OR omitted version specifiers are forbidden.

16. The integration-test file's `mod g8_plugin_manifests` block must use `tokio::process::Command` (not `std::process::Command`) for any helper subprocess invocations introduced by this WO — per WO-0075 §executor lesson W1 + WO-0076 pre-emptive carve-out. The existing `mcp_pytest_runner_manifest_health_check` uses `tokio::process::Command` already (via `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout` internally); the new test_runner test SHOULD follow the same minimalist shape. Pre-flight `! grep -qE 'std::process::Command' crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs` (covers both tests) before first push.

17. `unsafe std::env::set_var` IS FORBIDDEN in this WO's test code. test-runner-mcp does NOT need parent-process env-var mutation for the tools/list smoke. NEW unsafe blocks must NOT be introduced by this WO.

18. DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out applies to the augmentation of tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs (single small commit adding ONE test inside the existing `mod` block — likely <100 LOC). Cite DEC-0005 + WO-0080 commit precedent (ruff g7 augmentation) AND WO-0079 commit `c448dfc` precedent (graphiti g3 augmentation) in the relevant commit body. Do NOT split the new test into multiple commits.

19. §15.2 tracing spans do NOT apply to plugin-manifest landings — these features add data files (TOML manifests) + integration tests + helper scripts, NOT new orchestration code. Do NOT introduce new `#[tracing::instrument(...)]` annotations under this WO. Cite WO-0067 lessons §planner #2 ('pure-deterministic / data-only modules: §15.2 tracing does NOT apply') as precedent.

20. WO-0069 lessons §planner #2 carve-out (pre-emptive API-key-gate for plugins requiring keys) does NOT apply here — test-runner-mcp has NO API-key requirement. The verify script's gate is single-axis: `UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E` only.

21. WO-0069 lessons §planner #3 carve-out (eventual-consistency OR-disjunction): test-runner-mcp's tool-level smoke is fully synchronous — `tools/list` returns deterministic output on first invocation. AND-conjunctive assertions are fine. No OR-disjunction needed.

22. AC-set per WO-0070 lessons §planner #4: 'feat branch carries zero merge commits' (using `git log feat/WO-0082-test-runner-mcp-revival ^main --merges` returning empty), NOT the 'feat..main = 0' form.

23. Carry-forward standing protocol (37 WOs deep, WO-0058..WO-0080 + skipped WO-0081): coverage-gate.sh sccache RUSTC_WRAPPER workaround — `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --tests --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` is the authoritative measurement; the gate-script `coverage-gate.sh ucil-daemon 85 75` reporting `line=0%` is the known sccache interaction. Skip the `[FAIL] coverage gate: ucil-{core,daemon,embeddings}` lines in phase-3.sh output per scope_out + AC23 standing protocol.

24. Carry-forward standing protocol: AC30/AC31 phase-1/phase-2 effectiveness-gate flake (3 open escalations: 20260507T0357Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-rs-line-flake.md, 20260507T1629Z-effectiveness-refactor-rename-python-fixture-missing-symbol.md, 20260507T1930Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-doctest-caller-flake.md). Executor runs constituent cargo-test + clippy + MCP-smoke + Serena-docker + diagnostics-bridge sub-checks in isolation; verifier re-runs the gate-script entry point from a clean session. Pre-existing standing scope_out item; awaiting dedicated harness-improvement WO.

25. Verifier MUST NOT set `UCIL_SKIP_EXTERNAL_PLUGIN_TESTS` OR `UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E` envs (per WO-0076 scope_in #12 + WO-0079 scope_in #25 + WO-0080 scope_in #25 precedent extended to this WO).

26. RFR (ready-for-review) MUST include the M1/M2 mutation contract section with: (a) named file path, (b) the exact patch (e.g., `s/command = "npx"/command = "/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__"/`), (c) the targeted cargo-test selector, (d) the expected panic line, (e) the restore command (`git checkout -- <path>`), (f) the pre-mutation md5 snapshot path (`/tmp/wo-0082-<file>-orig.md5`). Mirror the WO-0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0077/0079/0080 RFR Mutation contract section verbatim in shape. The RFR must additionally include a §DEC-0025 chain-of-corrections section explicitly citing: (i) DEC-0021 deferral (superseded), (ii) DEC-0024 revival with material errors (amended), (iii) DEC-0025 corrected source data (this WO's source of truth), (iv) the executor's empirical-validation work in WO-0081 escalation that caught the errors.

27. Pre-flight `which npx` AND `which node` before first push — the integration tests will spawn from PATH, and missing `npx` produces false-positive `PluginError::Spawn { kind: NotFound }` which masks real M1 detection. Per WO-0069 lessons §verifier #2 + WO-0075/0076/0077/0079/0080 carry.

28. Pre-baked md5 snapshots: take `md5sum plugins/testing/test-runner/plugin.toml > /tmp/wo-0082-test-runner-orig.md5` AND `md5sum crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs > /tmp/wo-0082-g8tests-orig.md5` BEFORE applying any mutation. Confirm restoration via `md5sum -c /tmp/wo-0082-<name>-orig.md5` returning OK after each `git checkout --` restore.

29. WO-0072/0077/0079/0080 are the canonical references for the SOLO-FEATURE plugin-manifest WO shape. Same shape applied here: ONE feature, ONE manifest, ONE install script, ONE replacement verify script, ONE additive test in an existing peer test file (g8_plugin_manifests.rs), ONE M1 + ONE M2 mutation contract.

30. F07 has `attempts=0` per DEC-0025 §Consequences (the WO-0081 executor halt with empirical-validation escalation does NOT increment attempts; only verifier rejections do). This WO is the **first verifier-eligible attempt** for F07.

31. Apply WO-0076/0077/0080 §executor lessons #1-#5 verbatim: #1 (no kebab→snake translation; live capture documented in manifest comments — executor pins on the upstream's verbatim emission), #2 (no `--mcp` for warm-up; install script uses `npx ... --help`), #3 (`UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E` documented in test rustdoc + verify script — extends existing G8 env-var, NO new env-var needed), #4 (python deadline polling in verify script: 90s init / 30s tools-list / 60s tools-call), #5 (fixture copy BEFORE invoking — NA for tools/list path which needs no fixture; tool-level smoke optionally exercises the workspace's existing crates which are already test-friendly).

32. Npm package name vs serverInfo.name asymmetry per WO-0077 §planner lesson `Capture BOTH PyPI/npm package name AND serverInfo.name`: npm package = `@iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner`; runtime `serverInfo.name = "test-runner"` per DEC-0025 §Context. Document BOTH names verbatim in manifest top-of-file rustdoc. Manifest's `[plugin] name` field is `test-runner` (matches the runtime serverInfo.name); the install string references the npm package; the test asserts on `health.name == "test-runner"`.

33. Cold-cache npx fetch budget per WO-0077 §executor lesson: `FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS = 120_000` already set in tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs at line ~100 — covers the cold-cache npx fetch (resolves @iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner + @modelcontextprotocol/sdk + transitive deps + downloads npm tarballs) which takes up to ~30s; subsequent runs are <1s. NO change to FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS needed.

34. test-runner-mcp is SELF-RESOLVING at the MCP-server level per WO-0076/0077 §planner lesson. NO `resolve_working_test_runner()` helper, NO operator-state short-circuit on missing test-runner binary, NO `TEST_RUNNER_PATH` env var. The npx-managed package bundles all deps. The W1+W2 unsafe-set_var debt from WO-0076 dissolves naturally for this WO. NB: the SECONDARY operator-state CLI deps (cargo, pytest, vitest, go, bats, flutter, jest) are tool-call-time requirements ONLY — the cargo test (initialize-time tools/list) exercises NONE of them.

35. F07 spec acceptance per master-plan §4.8 + feature description: 'unified test execution covers Rust (cargo nextest), Python (pytest), and TypeScript (vitest) via MCP protocol.' Per DEC-0025 §Rationale: the functional spec is satisfied by the iflow-mcp `run_tests` dispatcher with `framework={rust, pytest, jest}` — the framework enum exposes ALL three required frameworks (and four more: bats, flutter, go, generic). The cargo-test asserts the tool's existence; the verify script's tool-level smoke confirms the framework enum exposes the three F07-required values. Tool-call-time end-to-end execution (`run_cargo_tests` etc.) is OPTIONAL informational-only via the cargo-version short-circuit per scope_in #11.

36. The DEC-0024 → DEC-0025 chain documents the executor empirical-validation pattern that caught two material errors in the planner research at zero cost. Cite this pattern in the manifest top-of-file rustdoc as a 'Provenance' section: (a) bare `test-runner-mcp` does NOT exist on npm (404) — only `@iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner` is published, (b) upstream advertises 1 tool with framework enum, NOT 6 separate tools — the framework enum was misread by the DEC-0024 author. This documentation prevents future planner regressions on the same upstream.

37. Phase 3 progress impact: with WO-0079 (graphiti) + WO-0080 (ruff) + WO-0082 (test-runner) all landing, the post-DEC-0022/0023/0024/0025 supersedure is fully discharged. Phase 3 reaches 21/45 = 46.7% complete after F07 lands. Subsequent planner passes (WO-0083+) MUST resume the regular Phase 3 feature-list traversal targeting the remaining 24 unfinished features (P3-W9-F11 incremental test suite, P3-W10-F01 Aider repo-map, P3-W10-F04 G5 Context query, P3-W10-F09..F18 dependency-cruiser/Zoekt + bonus-context selector + multi-tier merging + warm processors + architecture/dependencies/blast_radius MCP tools, P3-W11-F01/F05/F06 G7 quality pipeline + severity-merge + quality_issues table, P3-W11-F09..F16 G8 test discovery + check_quality + review_changes + feedback loop + integration test suites + type_check + full-pipeline integration suite). The deferral chain is administratively closed.
scope_out: 1. Do NOT implement a `TestRunnerG8Source` adapter for test-runner-mcp (deferred to a follow-up WO that wires the daemon-side plugin runtime against the F09 G8 test-discovery aggregator / F10 check_quality MCP tool).
2. Do NOT modify `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs` (WO-0044 P2-W6-F05/F06 regression guard) — keep that suite isolated per WO-0069 lessons §executor #2.

3. Do NOT modify the existing `mcp_pytest_runner_manifest_health_check` test inside `mod g8_plugin_manifests { ... }` — only ADD the second test_runner test. The leading file-level rustdoc may be amended ONLY to update the file overview per scope_in #8.

4. Do NOT add new G8 plugin manifests beyond test-runner under this WO (Bazel-runner, ts-runner, junit-runner, gradle-runner, etc. are all future-WO scope per master-plan §14.1 + DEC-0025 §Consequences).

5. Do NOT wire the new manifest into any `PluginManager::initialize_all` / startup-discovery path beyond what already exists — the WO-0044 + WO-0069 + WO-0076 + WO-0077 manifests are loaded on-demand by the integration tests via `PluginManifest::from_path(&manifest_path)`, and the new manifest should be loadable the same way without daemon-startup changes.
6. Do NOT introduce or modify any LLM-provider or embedding-provider configuration (test-runner-mcp has no LLM dependency).
7. Do NOT introduce graph-DB or external-service sidecar provisioning — test-runner-mcp is pure-Node multi-language test-runner shim, NO services beyond the npx-managed package.
8. Do NOT introduce any cross-WO dependency wiring (downstream WOs consume F07; that's their scope — F09/F10 G8 production-wiring deferrals).
9. Do NOT add any new `tracing::instrument` annotations to source code (per scope_in #19).
10. Do NOT add or modify entries in `ucil-build/feature-list.json` — verifier flips `passes` via `scripts/flip-feature.sh`.

11. Do NOT modify any file under `tests/fixtures/**` — verify scripts are read-only against the fixture (per WO-0069 forbidden_paths). Any tmpdir-fabrication for tool-level smoke lands in the `mktemp -d` tmpdir copy ONLY.

13. Do NOT pursue the Tier-2 vendored TS wrapper (DEC-0025 §Decision point 4 explicitly rejected Option B). If Tier-1 fails at executor time, halt and escalate — DO NOT silently pivot. A Tier-2 path requires a fresh ADR superseding DEC-0025.
14. Do NOT introduce a new `UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_*` env-var — extend the existing `UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E` from WO-0077 verbatim (single env-var per group; new tests reuse the existing gate).

15. Do NOT introduce `unsafe std::env::set_var` blocks (WO-0076 W1+W2 debt). test-runner-mcp is self-resolving at the MCP-server level — no parent-process env-var mutation needed (per scope_in #17 + #34).
16. Do NOT modify scripts/verify/coverage-gate.sh OR scripts/verify/effectiveness-gate.sh — the standing carry-forwards (37 WOs deep) remain out-of-scope.

17. Do NOT use the bare `test-runner-mcp` package name in any install command, manifest field, or RFR text — the bare name returns 404 from npm per DEC-0025 §Context Error 1. The ONLY published surface is `@iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner`.

18. Do NOT prescribe `health.tools.len() >= 6` (the DEC-0024 prescription that DEC-0025 amended). The correct assertion is `health.tools.len() == 1` AND `health.tools[0].name == "run_tests"` per DEC-0025 §Decision point 3.

19. Do NOT modify the seven peer integration-test files under `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g{1..7}_plugin_manifests.rs` AND `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs` — only `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs` is in-scope for additive test extension.

2. scripts/devtools/install-test-runner-mcp.sh exists, is executable (chmod +x), shells out to `npx -y @iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner@0.2.1 --help`, and exits 0 unconditionally (informational-only contract).

3. scripts/verify/P3-W11-F07.sh replaces the TODO-stub, exercises the cargo-test selector + an INDEPENDENT JSON-RPC `tools/list` capture asserting (a) `tools[]` len == 1, (b) `tools[0].name == "run_tests"`, (c) framework enum len ≥6 from canonical set {bats, pytest, flutter, jest, go, rust, cargo, generic, vitest}, and exits 0 on success / 1 on failure. Single-gate `UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E` short-circuit per scope_in #6.

4. crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs gains ONE new test `test_runner_manifest_health_check` inside the existing `mod g8_plugin_manifests { ... }` block; the test mirrors the existing test's shape and asserts on `health.name == "test-runner"`, `health.tools.len() == 1`, AND `health.tools[0].name == "run_tests"` (per DEC-0025 §Decision point 3).

5. cargo test selector `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g8_plugin_manifests g8_plugin_manifests::test_runner_manifest_health_check` resolves uniquely (substring-match without --exact), green from a `cargo clean` baseline.

8. M2 mutation (expected-tool-name regression) FAILS the cargo-test selector with the SA-tagged structured panic carrying the live `health.tools` array.

RFR file ucil-build/work-orders/0082-ready-for-review.md exists with: (a) summary, (b) M1/M2 mutation contracts in the canonical shape, (c) live `tools/list` capture transcript matching DEC-0025 §Context expectations, (d) DEC-0021 → DEC-0024 → DEC-0025 chain-of-corrections narrative per scope_in #26, (e) npm package name vs `serverInfo.name` documentation per WO-0077 §planner lesson, (f) explicit citation of WO-0081 escalation that drove the DEC-0025 corrections.

lessons_applied: DEC-0025 §Lessons-learned for planner: research-report claims about npm/PyPI publication MUST be verified with `npm view <pkg> version` BEFORE the ADR lands. This WO's expected literals (`@iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner@0.2.1`, single-tool dispatcher, framework enum 7 values) all derive from the WO-0081 executor's verified live capture per DEC-0025 §Context — NOT from web-search snippets.

WO-0080 lessons §planner: solo-feature G7 ruff revival template — copy the WO shape verbatim to G8 test-runner. Same approach: ONE feature, ONE manifest, ONE install script, ONE replacement verify script, ONE additive test, ONE M1 + ONE M2 mutation contract.

WO-0079 lessons §planner: deferral-revival pattern via fresh ADR (DEC-0022 graphiti, DEC-0023 ruff, DEC-0025 test-runner). Anchor each revival WO with the chain-of-revival cite (DEC-0021 → DEC-0024 → DEC-0025 here) so future planner regressions can verify which surface is canonical.

WO-0077 lessons §planner: PyPI/npm package name vs serverInfo.name asymmetry — capture both verbatim in the manifest top-of-file rustdoc when they differ. Here: npm = `@iflow-mcp/mcp-test-runner`, runtime = `serverInfo.name = "test-runner"`.

WO-0068 lessons §planner: frozen-test selector substring-match REQUIRES module-root placement. Pre-emptively prescribe `#[tokio::test] async fn` at module root inside the existing `mod g8_plugin_manifests { ... }` block.

WO-0070 lessons §planner #4: 'feat branch carries zero merge commits' wording (`git log feat/... ^main --merges`) NOT 'feat..main = 0' (tolerates 1-commit divergence between feat and main caused by gate-side critic-report commits).
WO-0067 lessons §planner #2: pure-deterministic / data-only modules — §15.2 tracing does NOT apply. No new `#[tracing::instrument]` annotations.

WO-0069 lessons §executor: production-side word-ban grep covers manifest+install+verify-script files; integration-test rustdoc 'Mocking forbidden' inside a forbidden-pattern declaration is exempt under WO-0048 line 363 carve-out.
